attributes.description: Fires when the worst `mountpoint` by inode `used` / (`used`+`free`) for each host exceeds 90% in the lookback. Complements the bytes-based filesystem rule. Tune 90.0 in ESQL.
attributes.name: [System Host Metrics OTel] Filesystem inode usage above critical threshold

investigation_guide.blob:
## Inode exhaustion

1. Many small files (build caches, mail queues) can fill inodes with space left.
2. The same `mountpoint` on the **bytes** and **inodes** dashboard panels should be compared.
3. On Linux, `df -i` is the direct analogue.

**Tune** Change `90.0` to your org standard.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-hostmetricsreceiver.otel-*
| WHERE `metrics.system.filesystem.inodes.usage` IS NOT NULL
| WHERE state IN ("used", "free")
| STATS u = MAX(CASE(state == "used", `metrics.system.filesystem.inodes.usage`, null)), f = MAX(CASE(state == "free", `metrics.system.filesystem.inodes.usage`, null)) BY `host.name`, mountpoint
| WHERE u IS NOT NULL AND f IS NOT NULL
| EVAL total_i = u + f
| WHERE total_i > 0
| EVAL used_inode_pct = u / to_double(total_i) * 100.0
| WHERE used_inode_pct > 90.0
| SORT used_inode_pct DESC
| STATS worst_inode_pct = MAX(used_inode_pct) BY `host.name`
| WHERE worst_inode_pct > 90.0
| SORT worst_inode_pct DESC
| LIMIT 20
